query: Considering a RAG system designed for continuous learning and handling novel queries via zero-shot techniques, how would the selection of a similarity metric (e.g., Cosine Similarity vs. Euclidean Distance) impact its performance and scalability when deployed on distributed vector databases utilizing sharding and replication for high availability?

chunks_used.content:
Continuous Learning And Adaptive Retrieval In RAG
Systems
Exploration of Few-Shot and Zero-Shot Learning in RAG
Application in RAG:
Enables handling of novel queries without prior
Created by Goyashy (@explainx.ai)
examples, crucial for RAG systems dealing with
new or unseen tasks. Zero-Shot
Learning
Techniques:
Uses pre-trained language models to interpret new
queries, supported by embedding-based retrieval to
match queries with relevant information.

Continuous Learning And Adaptive Retrieval In RAG
Systems
Exploration of Few-Shot and Zero-Shot Learning in RAG
Created by Goyashy (@explAaindx.aai)ptive Retrieval in Few-Shot and Zero-Shot Scenarios:
The retrieval component of a RAG system can be adapted to better
support few-shot and zero-shot learning by using similarity search
techniques that are robust to variations in the input. For example,
embedding-based retrieval methods, which represent both queries and
documents in a shared vector space, allow the system to retrieve relevant
information even when dealing with previously unseen queries.

Role In RAG Systems
Fast Similarity Search for Relevant Information Retrieval
Similarity Search: When a query is received, the RAG system generates a
query embedding. The vector database performs a fast similarity search,
comparing this embedding against the stored document embeddings. Created by Goyashy (@explainx.ai)
Nearest Neighbor Search: The database identifies the most
semantically similar documents or passages to the query, enabling
the retrieval of the most relevant information. Real-Time Performance: The speed and efficiency of vector databases ensure
that relevant documents can be retrieved quickly, supporting real-time
applications like chatbots, search engines, and recommendation systems.

Scaling And Deploying RAG Systems
Scaling Retrieval with Distributed Vector Databases
Vector Databases: Use distributed databases like Pinecone, Milvus,
or Weaviate for fast similarity searches and handling large-scale
vectors. Created by Goyashy (@explainx.ai)
Sharding: Distribute data across multiple shards or nodes to
enhance retrieval speed and enable horizontal scaling. Replication: Copy data across multiple nodes for high
availability and fault tolerance.

Metrics for Evaluating RAG Performance
BERTScore
Definition: BERTScore uses contextual embeddings from BERT to compare the semantic
similarity between the generated and reference texts, rather than focusing on exact word
matches. Created by Goyashy (@explainx.ai)
How it Works: It computes the cosine similarity between embeddings of the words in the
generated and reference texts to measure how close they are in meaning. Use Case: Useful for capturing the underlying meaning and context in tasks like abstractive
summarization and dialogue generation. BERTScore addresses the limitations of metrics
like BLEU and ROUGE by focusing on semantic similarity.
